title: 140  Why Chaitanya Mahaprabhu recommended Srimad Bhagavatam
speaker: HG Jai Chaitanya Dasa

segments.text: Chaitanya Mahaprabhu who made Srimad Bhagavatam his most important authoritative scripture
for his teaching, he says that relationship that we have with that ultimate source of
everything is not a passive relationship but it is a relationship of ecstasy. It's a very
strong, intimate, it is not a very distant relationship. Well just like there is a prime
minister of this country, we all know his name. Anything more we know about him, we
don't know. Can we say he is my close friend? No, we don't know, he is not our close friend.
We hardly know him, we see his picture in the newspaper, that's all. But here Bhagavatam
is giving us knowledge that the source of everything, the ultimate cause, who is the
cause of all prime ministers of all countries in the world, with him we have an intimate
relationship. And that relationship is an experience, understanding and acting at that
relationship is an experience of ecstasy, of great happiness. And Bhagavatam is giving
knowledge of that. Srimad Bhagavatam is the transcendental science, not only for knowing
the ultimate source of everything but also for knowing our relation with him so is that on two
things knowing the source of everything and our relation no one more thing is there bhagavatam
is going to teach what is that shimad bhagavatam is the transcendental science not only for knowing
the ultimate source of everything but also for knowing our relation with him and our duty
toward perfection of the human society on the basis of this perfect knowledge this knowledge
not only for knowing the ultimate source and our relationship and our duty each one of us have a
duty that is a duty in this human form of life our duty toward perfection of the human society
so the human society there are many types of cultures
that we can see in the world at different times in the in the in the history of the world they
had varieties of cultures but now we are talking about perfection of human society there is a
certain standard of perfection of human society and all of us have a duty towards that perfection
attainment of perfection so and our duty toward perfection of the human society and
what is the basis of this perfection of human society we are talking about on the basis of this
perfect knowledge perfect knowledge perfect knowledge of our relationship with that ultimate
source of it so we see in this one simple sentence Prabhupada has packed so much philosophical knowledge
and information which tells about the ultimate source of everything our relationship with him and
also tells us how we must act in this world
